root_cause.summary: apache/airflow's pre-commit `ts-compile-lint-ui` hook failed because `pnpm tsc --p <tmp tsconfig>` against the airflow UI sources reported 3 TypeScript errors that the project's strict TS config promotes to compile failures. The errors are: (1) `src/layouts/Nav/UserSettingsButton.tsx:55:6` TS6196 — `ColorMode` is declared (`type ColorMode = (typeof COLOR_MODES)[keyof typeof COLOR_MODES];`) but never used in the file; (2) `src/pages/Connections/ConnectionForm.tsx:33:1` TS6133 — `ParamsSpec` is imported (`import type { ParamsSpec } from "src/queries/useDagParams";`) but its value is never read; (3) `testsSetup.ts:78:3` TS2739 — `Type 'SetupServer' is missing the following properties from type 'SetupServerApi': #private, network` at the call site `server = setupServer(...(handlers as Array<HttpHandler>));`. The first two are dead-code warnings raised as errors under strict TS; the third is a genuine type-incompatibility error indicating an msw API or version drift between SetupServer and SetupServerApi. The Python prek helper (`scripts/ci/prek/ts_compile_lint_ui.py:62`) wraps the tsc invocation via `run_command`, sees the non-zero exit, and raises `subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '["pnpm", "tsc", "--p", "...tmpkhdkxx97.json"]' returned non-zero exit status 2`. Pre-commit then continues running ~30 OTHER hooks (Compile / format / lint simple auth manager UI, Generate the FastAPI API spec, etc., all of which Pass), so ~42% of the log AFTER the failure block is non-failure pre-commit chatter. The GHA step finally exits with `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1` at L3762 (≈58%). The diagnoser must navigate past the initial `Compile / format / lint UI...Failed` summary at L2069 (which alone identifies the failed hook but not the root cause) into the L3391-3479 tsc error block to identify the 3 specific TS errors and the type-incompatibility shape.

required_signals.value: src/layouts/Nav/UserSettingsButton.tsx:55:6 - error TS6196: 'ColorMode' is declared but never used.
src/pages/Connections/ConnectionForm.tsx:33:1 - error TS6133: 'ParamsSpec' is declared but its value is never read.
testsSetup.ts:78:3 - error TS2739: Type 'SetupServer' is missing the following properties from type 'SetupServerApi': #private, network
subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['pnpm', 'tsc', '--p', '/home/runner/work/airflow/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tmpkhdkxx97.json']' returned non-zero exit status 2.

evidence_spans.reason: Pre-commit summary line: `Compile / format / lint UI...Failed` + `hook id: ts-compile-lint-ui` + `exit code: 1` + `files were modified by this hook`. Identifies the failing pre-commit hook but does not by itself reveal the root cause (a reader who stops here might mistakenly conclude the failure is just an auto-formatter's file-modification gate).

The exact failing command: `Running command: pnpm tsc --p /home/runner/work/airflow/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tmpkhdkxx97.json`. Bridges the high-level hook name to the underlying tsc invocation.

First two TS errors (TS6196 ColorMode unused at UserSettingsButton.tsx:55, TS6133 ParamsSpec value never read at ConnectionForm.tsx:33) with code-context line snippets and ~~~ underscores. Establishes that the failure is multi-error, not a single bug.

Third TS error (TS2739 type incompatibility at testsSetup.ts:78): `Type 'SetupServer' is missing the following properties from type 'SetupServerApi': #private, network`. This is the genuine type-incompatibility error; the other two are dead-code warnings raised as errors. Critical for distinguishing 'unused imports' from 'real type bug'.

Test runner summary: `Found 3 errors in 3 files. Errors Files: 1 src/layouts/Nav/UserSettingsButton.tsx:55, 1 src/pages/Connections/ConnectionForm.tsx:33, 1 testsSetup.ts:78`. Self-contained — names all 3 affected files at file:line.

Python traceback wrapping the tsc exit: `scripts/ci/prek/ts_compile_lint_ui.py:62 → run_command → subprocess.check_call → subprocess.CalledProcessError: ... returned non-zero exit status 2`. Establishes the pre-commit-vs-tsc layering: a triager seeing only this block (without the L3391-3412 errors above) would not know which TS errors caused the exit.

GHA `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1`. Confirms the failure propagated to job exit code. Located at ≈58% of the log; ~42% of post-failure pre-commit chatter follows but adds no diagnostic information.
